message:
perf(mvcc): gate unregister_waiter on empty waiter_count

Adds a relaxed waiter_count load short-circuit to
`InProcessPageLockTable::unregister_waiter`. When the global waiter
count is zero, the per-shard `parking_lot::Mutex<HashMap>` acquisition
is skipped: the cumulative `fetch_add`/`fetch_sub` modification order
guarantees that observing zero means our entry has already been retired
from every shard.

Hits the post-park spurious-wake / re-acquire branch in
`wait_for_holder_change` (engine.rs:1041-1056), where the page-targeted
notifier (`notify_waiters_for_page`) already drained our entry inside
its own shard mutex with `unpark()` and `fetch_sub` both sequenced
before mutex release. By the time we resume from `park_timeout` and
arrive at `unregister_waiter`, the gate observes the post-drain zero
and returns without contending the same shard mutex the notifier just
released.

The relaxed load is safe by abstract-state argument: a stale non-zero
observation only forces the (always-correct) slow path; a zero
observation is a global-modification-order invariant that no waiter
entry — including ours — remains in any shard.

Microbench (`bench_unregister_waiter_drained`, n=7 trials, same-run
toggle via `waiter_count.store(1)` per slow iter to force the slow
body with no map entry, mirroring `bench_notify_waiters_no_waiters`):

  fast median 2.1 ns/call (gate hits)
  slow median 5.1 ns/call (gate misses, takes shard mutex)
  delta -58.9%

Closes 0.14% MT8 self-time residual on `mt-mvcc-bench`
(`docs/perf-mvcc-session-20260424.md`).

Correctness: 1345 pass / 7 fail with patch — identical pre-existing
failures to stashed baseline (`shared_lock_table`, `lifecycle x3`,
`history_compression`, `core_types::test_cleanup_orphaned_slots_logs_*`,
`begin_concurrent::test_zero_witness_commit_stays_candidate_free_with_active_peer`)
from concurrent-agent mid-work. All `wait_for_holder_change` tests
pass: wakes_on_release, times_out.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
